Petition For Court Order To Distribute To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for and with Alexander Sutton (Trustee) by zoom.
Christopher Land appeared by zoom for Lura Sims (Petitioner).

The Court asked about the relationship of the Texas property to the Trust. Mr. Land stated that the trust was one of quite a few owners of the Texas property, that the Parties executed a notarized agreement to hold the sale proceeds in escrow, that the trust became a joint owner many years ago and that the trust's share is $300,000.00. Ms. Ebey stated that she believes the property was inherited by the Settlor and that the property filtered down, but she is not sure when it happened. The Court raised the issue of jurisdiction and venue, stated that in 2003 this trust was created by substituted judgment in this Court, that in April of 2008 this Court ordered that it would retain jurisdiction and that in June of 2013 this Court ordered transfer of this trust proceeding to New Jersey. The Court also stated that in October of 2013 even though it had been ordered to be transferred, Judge Wiss instead ordered distribution, and questions why distribution wasn't done and why the property wasn't liquidated. Ms. Ebey stated that she does not believe the asset was known at the time. Mr. Land stated that he thinks there were about a dozen Beneficiaries and that from what he can see, the Trustee was resistant in making those distributions. Mr. Sutton stated that at the time there were four additional properties in the Aunt's estate that were not considered by the California Court, that he couldn't liquidate until all debts were resolved and that he is not aware of any other assets. The Court stated that Mr. Sutton needed to follow the Court's order, and that if Mr. Sutton thought the order was wrong, he was to appeal it and not ignore it. The Court also stated that the Court's intent was clearly to shut down this trust, that instead it is before this Court again and that it thinks the petition is properly before it. Ms. Ebey stated there is opposition to the substantive request, that the petition seeks to bypass trust administration, that it prevents the trust Administrator from filing documents and that there will be a capital gains tax due. Ms. Ebey also stated that the Petitioner as asking for her distribution to be made prior to payment of any capital gains tax, that her Client has to answer to the IRS and will have to pay expenses, and that the agreement was to wait until there was an unappealable order of this Court. Discussion was held as to distribution percentages. The Court raised the issue of letting the Parties work out the percentages and the issue whether they are to be distributed after collection of costs. The First Amended Petition for Court Order to Distribute to Beneficiaries is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Carranza. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-18-2012 Continued To Nov-06-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laura Spease for Alexander Sutton, Jr. Attorney Joseph Lambariello Administrator of the Estate in New Jersey appeared by telephone. Lura Sutton-Sims appaered by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Lambariello stated that there was an offer made to purchase both properties (Texas) for $100,000.00 but that they want seller help financing, and that with this number they can file the inheritance tax in New Jersey. In addition, Mr. Lambariello stated that the only thing left to do is to sell the property, do the taxes, that there is nothing left in California, that his fees (legal fees not Administrator's fees) are subject to New Jersey court approval and that he has not spoken to Alex about his fees. The Court stated that Alex has to either waive his fees or amend the petition to request his fees, and that it would like to see something filed with respect to said fees. Ms. Spease stated that the final account will terminate the Trust in California. Mr. Lambariello also stated that New Jersey will have jurisdiction over the Trust and that jurisdiction lies in New Jersey because the California has been sold. Ms. Sutton-Sims presented her objections to the accounting, raised the issue of the unclaimed property not being accounted for and argued that jurisdiction should remain in California. The accounting petition was continued to November 6, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will ascertain whether this portion of the Trust can conclude in California. The Court also needs something specific with regard to the Trustee's fees (waiving/requesting). Mr. Lambariello provided the Court with his fax number and e-mail address.
